channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Biomarkers & Precision Medicine
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Biomarkers & Precision Medicine

item.title: Cytomegalovirus infection drives a distinct mucosal and systemic cytokine signature in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Virology journal - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease in which cytomegalovirus (CMV) reactivation has been linked to severe and steroid-refractory cases. However, the effect of CMV on cyt...

item.title: Development and validation of a cross-species breath metabolomics platform for translational VOC analysis.
item.description: Analytical methods : advancing methods and applications - The lack of universal cross-species sampling and quality control methods has limited the potential of breath metabolomics to advance from clinical discovery to mechanistic validation. To address th...

item.title: Sub-inhibitory concentrations of metronidazole alter the proteomic profile and biological effects of extracellular vesicles from Clostridioides difficile.
item.description: BMC microbiology - Colonization with C. difficile is common in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The use of antibiotics can trigger the transition from colonization to active infection, complicating IBD...

item.title: Self-adaptive nanozyme-loaded yeast microcapsules for multifunctional and precise therapy of colitis via intestinal microenvironment remodeling.
item.description: Journal of nanobiotechnology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) management remains a significant challenge due to its complex pathogenesis. While current therapies for IBD are effective in symptom management, a radical cure rema...

item.title: Multidimensional dissection of shared genetic susceptibility in ulcerative colitis and colorectal cancer: novel insights from integrative single-cell and multi-omics analysis.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - Ulcerative colitis (UC) patients carry a 2.5-fold increased risk of colorectal cancer (CRC), yet the shared multi-scale genetic architecture remains poorly understood. We constructed an integrative...

item.title: Aimed at Subtype Discrimination but Yielding a Shared Marker: Integrative Analysis of Blood Transcriptomes Reveals Upregulated TLR5 as a Potential Biomarker for IBD.
item.description: Clinical and experimental gastroenterology - Despite the similarities, Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), the two major subtypes of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), exhibit distinctions. The increasing burden of IBD necessitat...

item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease and renal disorders: from clinical associations to shared mechanisms and management strategies.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - A growing body of evidence indicates a strong relationship between inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and a range of renal conditions, including chronic kidney disease, glomerulonephritis, tubulointe...
